ddls:
CREATE TABLE "social_media_platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the social media platform (e.g., Facebook, Twitter) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the platform */,
  "api_endpoint" TEXT /* URL of the API endpoint for the social media platform */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key required to access the platform's data */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the platform is active or not */,
  "last_sync_date" TEXT /* Date of the last data synchronization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the engagement metric (e.g., likes, comments, shares) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., count, percentage) */,
  "aggregation_type" TEXT /* Type of aggregation (e.g., sum, average) */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the metric is active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weekly_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* ID of the social media platform */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engagement metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the engagement metric */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id", "platform_id", "metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "facebook_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "likes" REAL /* Number of likes */,
  "comments" REAL /* Number of comments */,
  "shares" REAL /* Number of shares */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "twitter_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "tweets" REAL /* Number of tweets */,
  "replies" REAL /* Number of replies */,
  "retweets" REAL /* Number of retweets */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flickr_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "views" REAL /* Number of views */,
  "comments" REAL /* Number of comments */,
  "favorites" REAL /* Number of favorites */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gmail_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "emails_sent" REAL /* Number of emails sent */,
  "emails_received" REAL /* Number of emails received */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "picasa_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "views" REAL /* Number of views */,
  "comments" REAL /* Number of comments */,
  "likes" REAL /* Number of likes */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instagram_data" (
  "week_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each week */,
  "week_date" TEXT /* Date of the week */,
  "posts" REAL /* Number of posts */,
  "likes" REAL /* Number of likes */,
  "comments" REAL /* Number of comments */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number to track changes over time */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("week_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_data" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the client belongs to */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact person for the client */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the primary contact person */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the client data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the client data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the client data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the client data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the client data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or device used to access the client data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_access_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES client_data ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
